title: myrstiphyllum horizontalis
intro: Myrstiphyllum horizontalis, also known as the Horsetail or Equisetum, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the class Equisetopsida. This ancient plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat urinary tract infections due to its high silica content. Horsetail is also valued for its health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. Horsetail can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or capsule, and is often used in herbal remedies to support kidney and bladder health, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
